header: 3LangFlow: Continuous Language Modeling via Flow Matching

images.caption:
Figure 1:LangFlow takes noisy token embeddings as inputs and predicts clean probabilities.In thetraining (Left), we first map discrete token sequence𝒙{\bm{x}}to token embeddings𝒛{\bm{z}}via a learnable embedding matrix𝐄{\mathbf{E}}.
Then, we perturb𝒛{\bm{z}}into noisy embeddings𝒛γ{\bm{z}}_{\gamma}according toγ\gamma, the sampled logarithmic noise-to-signal ratio.
Finally, we use a denoiser network to predict categorical distribution𝒙^𝜽​(𝒛γ,γ)\hat{\bm{x}}_{\bm{\theta}}({\bm{z}}_{\gamma},\gamma)and supervise this prediction with the cross-entropy loss.
Self-conditioning is randomly activated.
In thesampling (Right), we start from Gaussian noise𝒛γ0{\bm{z}}_{\gamma_{0}}and iteratively denoise it towards clean token embeddings𝒛γN{\bm{z}}_{\gamma_{N}}.
We define the moving target𝒛^𝜽\hat{{\bm{z}}}_{\bm{\theta}}for every step by embedding predicted clean probabilities𝒙^𝜽\hat{\bm{x}}_{\bm{\theta}}with𝐄{\mathbf{E}}.
The final token sequence is obtained byarg⁡max\arg\maxdecoding of𝒙^𝜽​(𝒛γN,γN)\hat{\bm{x}}_{\bm{\theta}}({\bm{z}}_{\gamma_{N}},\gamma_{N}).

header: 4Improved Design Choices of Continuous Diffusion Language Models

images.caption: Figure 2:Loss geometry.(Left) Training loss as a function of the OT flow matching timet=sigmoid⁡(γ/2)t=\operatorname{sigmoid}(\gamma/2). (Middle) Loss overγ=log⁡NSR\gamma=\log\mathrm{NSR}reveals a consistent geometry across training stages. (Right) The derivative∂ℒ/∂γ\partial\mathcal{L}/\partial\gammaconcentrates in a narrow region, forming a stable structure.

Figure 3:Comparison of Nearest Neighbor Distance (NND) among embeddings of four language modeling architectures: autoregressive (AR), MDLM(Sahooet al.,2024), Plaid(Gulrajani and Hashimoto,2023), and LangFlow. Compared to the other three architectures, the NNDs of Plaid cluster around zero, indicating the indistinguishability of different embeddings (i.e., mode collapse).

Figure 4:Self-conditioning dynamics on LM1B. The clean token is “run”. Dashed lines denote pass 1 (without SC), and solid lines denote pass 2 (with SC). We visualize the evolution ofp​(x∣zγ)p(x\mid z_{\gamma})across token groups.
